_comment: 7-event golden dataset. UUIDs are fixed — never regenerate. Org IDs from orgs.json.

events.title: Birmingham Small Business Week 2026
events.description: Birmingham's premier small business and entrepreneurship conference. Three days of sessions covering business growth, technology adoption, and community investment — featuring local founders, corporate sponsors, and civic leaders.

speakers.name: Jordan Mercer
speakers.bio: Serial entrepreneur and founder of BizForward, a small business accelerator serving under-resourced communities across the Southeast. Advocate for equitable access to capital and mentorship.

speakers.name: Dr. Alicia Tate
speakers.bio: Director of the MBA Entrepreneurship Program at UAB and researcher specializing in minority-owned business development and CDFI lending.

speakers.name: Marcus Webb
speakers.bio: Digital marketing strategist who has helped 200+ small businesses build online presence. Founder of Main Street Digital, a Birmingham-based agency.

speakers.name: Simone Hayes
speakers.bio: Three-time founder and e-commerce consultant. Built and exited two online retail businesses serving the Southern US market. Now advises brick-and-mortar retailers on digital transformation.

speakers.name: Rev. Thomas Bridges
speakers.bio: Community development director at New Covenant CDC, connecting faith communities with economic development resources across Jefferson County.

speakers.name: Priya Patel
speakers.bio: Fintech entrepreneur and founder of Lendwise, a platform connecting small business owners with alternative financing. Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree.

speakers.name: Kevin Rodriguez
speakers.bio: Digital marketing consultant specializing in AI-assisted campaigns for Main Street businesses. Former Google SMB advisor.

speakers.name: Naomi Washington
speakers.bio: Grant writer and nonprofit capacity builder who has secured over $12M in federal, state, and foundation grants for small businesses and community organizations.

sessions.title: Opening Keynote: Building Wealth in the New Economy
sessions.description: A call to action for Birmingham's small business community — examining generational wealth-building, access to capital, and the economic opportunity ahead.

sessions.title: Building Bankable Credit for Small Business
sessions.description: Practical steps for establishing and rebuilding business credit, separating personal and business finances, and approaching lenders with confidence.

sessions.title: Digital Marketing Masterclass for Main Street
sessions.description: Hands-on workshop: build a 90-day digital presence plan using social media, email, and low-cost ad tools tailored for local businesses.

sessions.title: Community Capital: Raising Local Investment
sessions.description: Panelists discuss community investment structures, equity crowdfunding, and how faith communities and CDFIs are bridging the financing gap for Birmingham entrepreneurs.

sessions.title: AI Tools for the Small Business Owner
sessions.description: Practical demonstrations of AI-assisted tools for scheduling, customer service, social media, and competitive research — no technical background needed.

sessions.title: Minority-Owned Business Certification Workshop
sessions.description: Step-by-step guide to applying for MBE, WBE, DBE, and SBA certifications — including documentation requirements, timelines, and how to leverage certifications for contracts.

sessions.title: Networking Lunch
sessions.description: Open networking lunch for all attendees, exhibitors, and sponsors.

sessions.title: The Future of Downtown Commerce
sessions.description: Panelists examine how mixed-use development, transit investment, and anchor institutions are reshaping economic opportunity in Birmingham's neighborhoods.

sessions.title: E-Commerce Strategies for Local Retailers
sessions.description: From Shopify setup to marketplace listings to local delivery fulfillment — a practical roadmap for brick-and-mortar businesses entering online retail.

sessions.title: Grant Writing for Small Businesses
sessions.description: Workshop covering federal and state grant programs available to small businesses, how to write winning applications, and common disqualifying mistakes to avoid.

sessions.title: Building Supplier Diversity Programs That Work
sessions.description: Corporate procurement leaders and small business advocates discuss what supplier diversity programs actually deliver — and how certified businesses can break in.

sessions.title: Closing Keynote & Small Business Awards
sessions.description: Closing celebration recognizing outstanding Birmingham small businesses, community champions, and rising entrepreneurs, followed by networking reception.

events.title: SAUP Annual CE Conference 2026
events.description: The Southeast Association of Urban Planners' annual continuing education conference. Four days of AICP-certified sessions spanning policy, practice, and design — delivered in person and via live stream.

speakers.name: Dr. Raymond Foster, AICP
speakers.bio: Professor of urban planning at Georgia Tech with a focus on climate resilience policy in the Southeast. Author of two books on urban heat and infrastructure adaptation.

speakers.name: Keiko Yamamoto
speakers.bio: Sustainable design consultant specializing in transit-oriented development and mixed-income housing. Past projects include multiple HUD Choice Neighborhoods grantees.

speakers.name: Dr. Antoine Beaumont
speakers.bio: Housing policy researcher and former HUD senior advisor. Expert in affordable housing finance, inclusionary zoning, and community land trusts.

speakers.name: Christina Blackwell, AICP
speakers.bio: City planner with 15 years experience in form-based code development. Led the Birmingham Complete Neighborhoods code overhaul in 2023.

speakers.name: Marcus Thompson
speakers.bio: Transportation planner and Complete Streets advocate. Has managed street redesigns in four major Southern cities, reducing pedestrian fatalities by 40%.

speakers.name: Dr. Priscilla Osei
speakers.bio: Regional planning director for the Atlanta Regional Commission. Leads coordination of multi-jurisdictional plans for the 11-county ARC region.

sessions.title: Urban Climate Resilience Policy
sessions.description: Examining FEMA, HUD, and state-level policy frameworks for climate adaptation — and how local planners can align comprehensive plans with resilience mandates. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

sessions.title: Equitable Transit-Oriented Development Workshop
sessions.description: Hands-on workshop: anti-displacement strategies, community benefit agreements, and affordable housing preservation in the context of TOD. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

sessions.title: Affordable Housing Finance Policy
sessions.description: Panel examining LIHTC, HOME, and local trust fund programs — what's working, what's under threat, and how planners can advocate for stronger pipelines. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

sessions.title: Form-Based Codes in Practice
sessions.description: Case study walkthrough of Birmingham's recent form-based code adoption: stakeholder process, calibration, and implementation lessons for other municipalities. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

sessions.title: Complete Streets Implementation
sessions.description: From policy adoption to on-the-ground construction: a panel discussion on navigating political, engineering, and community challenges in Complete Streets projects. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

sessions.title: Urban Parks and Green Infrastructure Workshop
sessions.description: Workshop: applying green infrastructure standards, park equity analysis, and CDBG funding to improve urban parks in underserved neighborhoods. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

sessions.title: Regional Planning Coordination
sessions.description: How multi-jurisdictional regional agencies coordinate comprehensive plans, housing targets, and infrastructure investment across county lines. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

sessions.title: Mixed-Use Development Design Workshop
sessions.description: Studio-style workshop using real Birmingham sites to apply mixed-use design principles, zoning variance processes, and urban design review criteria. 1.0 AICP CM credit.

form_fields.label: I confirm I am eligible for AICP CE credit

events.title: Augusta Metro Chamber Spring Mixer 2026
events.description: The Augusta Metro Chamber's summer networking mixer — an evening of connection, conversation, and community for Augusta's business and civic leaders.
events.venue_name: Augusta Marriott at the Convention Center

speakers.name: Mayor William Garrett
speakers.bio: Mayor of Augusta, Georgia. Focused on economic development, workforce training, and attracting technology investment to the Augusta region.

speakers.name: Jennifer Walters
speakers.bio: Director of Economic Development for Augusta. Leads business attraction, retention, and workforce development initiatives for the region.

speakers.name: Robert Kim
speakers.bio: Chair of the Augusta Metro Chamber Board of Directors and owner of three Augusta-area businesses. Champion of Buy Local Augusta initiatives.

sessions.title: Welcome Reception & Open Networking
sessions.description: Cocktail-style open networking with heavy hors d'oeuvres. Mix with business leaders, elected officials, and Chamber members from across the Augusta region.

sessions.title: Augusta's Economic Momentum: What's Next
sessions.description: Chamber leadership and city officials discuss Augusta's 2026 economic development pipeline, key business wins, and priorities for the region's future.

events.title: OpenSource ATL May Meetup 2026
events.description: Monthly Atlanta open source and developer community meetup. Two technical talks and open hacking time. Casual, free, all skill levels welcome.

speakers.name: Dmitri Volkov
speakers.bio: Senior software engineer at GitHub and active open source contributor with 150+ merged PRs across CNCF projects. Specializes in distributed systems tooling.

speakers._note: No-photo speaker — exercises the photo-vs-no-photo badge split case
speakers.name: Taylor Kim
speakers.bio: Developer advocate and open source educator. Runs the 'Contribute First' workshop series helping developers make their first open source PR.

sessions.title: Open Source Licensing Demystified
sessions.description: A plain-English breakdown of MIT, Apache 2.0, GPL, and AGPL — when to use each, how they interact, and what enterprise contributors need to know.

sessions.title: How to Land Your First Contribution to a Major OSS Project
sessions.description: Practical walk-through: finding good first issues, setting up local environments, writing good commit messages, and navigating the PR review process.

sessions.title: Community Hackathon & Open Networking
sessions.description: Bring a project, find collaborators, or just hang out. Open hacking with pizza and drinks.

events.title: OpenSource ATL Virtual Summit 2026
events.description: OpenSource ATL's flagship annual virtual summit — three days of keynotes, deep-dive workshops, and panels covering infrastructure, tooling, community, and the future of open source. Free for all, globally accessible.

speakers.name: Sunita Raghavan
speakers.bio: Engineering Director at the Linux Foundation overseeing CNCF graduation programs. Former principal architect at Red Hat. Frequent OSS keynote speaker.

speakers.name: Lars Eriksson
speakers.bio: Rust programming language core contributor and author of two O'Reilly books on systems programming. 20-year career in infrastructure and compiler design.

speakers.name: Aaliyah Thompson
speakers.bio: NumFOCUS Program Director focused on diversity and inclusion in the scientific Python ecosystem. Creator of the OSS Onramp Fellowship program.

speakers.name: Daniel Park
speakers.bio: CPython core developer and PyCon US program committee chair. Contributes primarily to CPython's async infrastructure and packaging toolchain.

speakers.name: Sam Okonkwo
speakers.bio: Platform Engineering Lead at a major SaaS company. Architect of internal developer platforms serving 2,000+ engineers. Active contributor to Backstage and Crossplane.

speakers.name: Grace Chen
speakers.bio: OSS funding strategist and former GitHub director. Advises foundations and corporations on open source investment, SBOM compliance, and sustainable maintenance models.

speakers.name: Felipe Navarro
speakers.bio: CNCF Ambassador and Kubernetes contributor. DevRel engineer helping LATAM developers break into cloud-native open source.

speakers.name: Morgan Williams
speakers.bio: Security researcher and co-founder of OSS-Sec Advisory, focused on supply chain attacks, SBOM tooling, and responsible disclosure in open source ecosystems.

speakers.name: Takeshi Yamada
speakers.bio: Linux kernel maintainer of the SCSI subsystem and long-time contributor to the kernel's block layer. Works at Fujitsu's OSS Technology Center.

speakers.name: Isabelle Tremblay
speakers.bio: Director of Developer Experience at Vercel and creator of the DX Maturity Framework. Speaks on documentation, onboarding, and SDK design.

speakers.name: Raj Patel
speakers.bio: Zero-trust security architect who has open-sourced several policy engines and auth libraries. Co-author of the NIST Zero Trust roadmap for OSS organizations.

speakers.name: Vera Kozlov
speakers.bio: Distributed systems engineer and contributor to etcd, Vitess, and TiKV. Runs the Distributed Systems Reading Group Atlanta.

sessions.title: Opening Keynote: The Next Wave of Open Source
sessions.description: How AI-assisted development, SBOM mandates, and enterprise adoption are reshaping what open source means in 2026 — and what opportunities that creates for contributors and maintainers.

sessions.title: Rust for Systems Developers in 2026
sessions.description: What's new in Rust's async ecosystem, the road to Rust in the Linux kernel, and practical patterns for adopting Rust in systems programming projects.

sessions.title: Building Maintainable Open Source Communities
sessions.description: What makes OSS communities last 10+ years? Governance models, contributor ladders, burnout prevention, and the metrics that actually matter.

sessions.title: Container Orchestration Beyond Kubernetes: Nomad Workshop
sessions.description: Hands-on workshop: deploying workloads with Nomad, comparing Nomad and Kubernetes operator models, and integrating with Vault and Consul.

sessions.title: Funding Open Source: Models That Actually Work
sessions.description: Panel examining sponsorship tiers, foundation fiscal sponsorship, commercial open core, and the reality of GitHub Sponsors — with honest numbers from real maintainers.

sessions.title: Deep Dive: Linux Kernel Contributions
sessions.description: How to contribute to the Linux kernel: subsystem selection, working with mailing lists, coding style, and the patch review process from a maintainer's perspective.

sessions.title: Developer Experience in the Age of AI
sessions.description: How AI coding assistants are changing what good DX means — from documentation to error messages to onboarding. Designing for human+AI developer workflows.

sessions.title: Zero-Trust Security for OSS Infrastructure
sessions.description: Applying zero-trust principles to open source project infrastructure: CI/CD hardening, secrets management, dependency pinning, and SLSA compliance.

sessions.title: WebAssembly Workshop: Beyond the Browser
sessions.description: Hands-on workshop: running Wasm workloads server-side with Wasmtime, WASI, and component model. Build and deploy a real Wasm-based service.

sessions.title: Observability and OSS: OpenTelemetry in Production
sessions.description: Distributed tracing, metrics, and logging with OpenTelemetry. Real-world patterns from teams running OTel at scale in Kubernetes environments.

sessions.title: Emerging Leaders in Open Source
sessions.description: Panel: first-generation open source contributors from underrepresented communities share their paths, the barriers they encountered, and what made the difference.

sessions.title: Platform Engineering Workshop: Build Your Internal Developer Platform
sessions.description: Hands-on: stand up a Backstage-based IDP with a software catalog, scaffolder templates, and a Tech Radar. Participants leave with a working local deployment.

sessions.title: Building Developer Tools That Last Workshop
sessions.description: Workshop: designing CLI ergonomics, SDK versioning, changelog automation, and feedback loops that turn users into contributors.

sessions.title: FOSS Licensing for Enterprises
sessions.description: How large organizations navigate GPL copyleft, AGPL network provisions, and SSPL restrictions when building on open source foundations.

sessions.title: The Business of Open Source: Revenue Models and Sustainability
sessions.description: Hard-won lessons on open core, managed services, enterprise support tiers, and consulting models — from founders who have done each.

events.title: SAUP Winter Planning Workshop 2026
events.description: A full-day professional development workshop for SAUP members focused on emerging planning practice topics. Draft — program and registration TBD.

speakers.name: Dr. Patricia Okonkwo
speakers.bio: Executive Director of the Southeast Association of Urban Planners. Research focus on equitable development in Southern US cities.

speakers.name: James Chen, AICP
speakers.bio: Visiting professor and former director of the APA Georgia chapter. Specializes in form-based codes and smart growth in the South.

speakers.name: Dr. Maya Rodriguez
speakers.bio: Housing policy researcher at the Urban Land Institute. Expert in affordable housing policy, community land trusts, and anti-displacement strategies.

speakers.name: Kevin Barber, AICP
speakers.bio: Urban redevelopment consultant with 20 years of experience on brownfield remediation, TIF financing, and public-private development partnerships.

sessions.title: Introduction to Form-Based Zoning Codes
sessions.description: Overview of form-based code principles, national examples, and application in Southern US municipalities.

sessions.title: Urban Redevelopment Finance Strategies
sessions.description: TIF districts, opportunity zones, brownfield grants, and public-private partnership structures for urban redevelopment projects.

sessions.title: Inclusionary Housing Policy Analysis
sessions.description: Comparative analysis of inclusionary zoning ordinances — set-aside requirements, in-lieu fees, and community land trust integration.

sessions.title: Smart City Infrastructure Planning
sessions.description: Fiber broadband, smart streetlights, traffic management systems, and data governance in small-to-mid-size Southern US cities.

events.title: Augusta Metro Annual Business Gala 2026
events.description: Annual business recognition gala for Augusta Metro Chamber members. Event cancelled — refunds processed.
events.venue_name: Augusta Marriott at the Convention Center
